seed.description: Direct read-after-write test. A communication-style preference (bullet lists for any structured response) is established at turn 5. Subsequent open-ended questions (turns 13, 16, 19) probe whether the agent actually applies the preference in its replies, not just whether it wrote it to the profile.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Walk me through how to set up a CI pipeline.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Sure. Which platform — GitHub Actions, GitLab, CircleCI?

dialogue_rounds.user_message: GitHub Actions.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: OK, GitHub Actions it is.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: What goes in the workflow file?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: A workflow file lives in .github/workflows/. It defines triggers (push, PR, schedule), jobs (groups of steps run on a runner), and steps (individual commands or actions).

dialogue_rounds.user_message: What runners are available?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: GitHub provides ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, and macos-latest by default. You can also use self-hosted runners.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Quick style preference for everything we do: when you give me anything structured — steps, options, comparisons, lists — always use bullet lists. Don't give me prose paragraphs for structured info.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — bullet lists for any structured info from now on.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: What are the key parts of a workflow step?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- name: descriptive label
- uses: action reference
- run: shell command
- with: action inputs
- env: env vars

dialogue_rounds.user_message: How does caching work?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- actions/cache@v4 stores a path keyed by an input
- Restore key falls back if exact key misses
- Cache scope is branch + repo

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Common pitfalls?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- Cache key not invalidated when deps change
- Not pinning action versions
- Secrets leaking via echo or env
- Slow runners on Windows / macOS
- Matrix builds blowing up minutes

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Let me see a sample test job.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: (provides sample yaml)

dialogue_rounds.user_message: How does this run on PRs vs push?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- on: push triggers on direct push to specified branches
- on: pull_request triggers on PR open / sync
- on: pull_request_target runs in base repo's context (security implications)

dialogue_rounds.user_message: What about deploying after CI passes?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- Use a separate deploy job with `needs: test`
- Gate on environment with manual approval
- Use OIDC to AWS / GCP instead of long-lived secrets
- Lock production deploys to main branch

dialogue_rounds.user_message: How do I debug a failed run?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- Open the run page
- Click the failing step to expand logs
- Re-run with debug logging via ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG
- SSH into runner via tmate action for live debug
- Compare with the last passing run

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Compare GitHub Actions to GitLab CI for me.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: PROBE — should reply in bullets, not prose.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: What versions of Node should I test against?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- Active LTS
- Previous LTS
- Current
- Drop EOL versions

dialogue_rounds.user_message: How do I parallelize tests?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- Matrix strategy splits across runners
- Sharding with --shard flag
- Test reporters merge results
- Cap fail-fast to spot all breakages

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Walk me through the security best practices.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: PROBE — multi-item topic, must be bullets.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Action versioning best practice?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- Pin to commit SHA, not tag
- Tag is mutable, SHA is not
- Use Dependabot to update SHAs
- Trust only verified publishers

dialogue_rounds.user_message: How to monitor CI minutes?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
- Org settings > Actions > Usage
- Set monthly spending limits
- Alert on threshold via webhook
- Optimize by caching deps

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Give me a summary of everything I should keep in mind setting this up.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: PROBE — open question, structured response, must be bullets.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Thanks.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Anytime.

qa_items.question: When I ask for structured information, what format do I want?
